name: Tomato Chutney
description: Tomato Chutney or Thakkali Chutney is a very popular South Indian side dish made with ripe tomatoes, chillies and garlic. It is tangy, mildly sweet and spicy — a perfect pair for idli, dosa, paniyaram, masala dosa, or even as a spread for sandwiches. Cooking the tomatoes down until mushy and then tempering gives this chutney a deep, rich flavour.

steps.instruction: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan. Add urad dal and dry red chillies. Sauté till golden.
steps.tip: Urad dal can be substituted with chana dal, flax seeds or sesame seeds.

steps.instruction: Add chopped tomatoes, salt and sugar. Mix well.
steps.tip: Use ripe red tomatoes which are a little sour for the best taste.

steps.instruction: Cover and simmer for 5 to 10 minutes until tomatoes are completely cooked and mushy.
Cool the mixture completely, then transfer to a blender and puree till smooth.
For tempering, heat 4 tbsp oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
steps.tip: Use gingelly oil for maximum authentic flavour.

steps.instruction: Add curry leaves and asafoetida. Let them sizzle.
Pour the tomato puree into the tempering oil. Add a splash of water if needed.
Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the chutney thickens and oil separates on the sides.
steps.tip: Cooking till oil separates helps preserve the chutney longer without refrigeration.

steps.instruction: Remove from heat and serve hot or at room temperature.
steps.tip: Store in the refrigerator for up to a week.

tips: Use ripe red tomatoes which are a little sour for the best taste.
Use gingelly oil for maximum authentic flavour.
Urad dal can be substituted with chana dal, flax seeds or sesame seeds.
Garlic can be added for extra depth of flavour.
Cooking till oil separates helps preserve the chutney longer without refrigeration.
Store in the refrigerator for up to a week.
